title: n8n Webhook node IP whitelist bypass via substring includes() matching
description: The isIpWhitelisted() function in the Webhook node uses JavaScript's String.includes() for IP address matching instead of exact comparison or CIDR-aware matching. This allows an attacker with IP 11.2.3.4 to bypass a whitelist entry of 1.2.3.4 because '11.2.3.4'.includes('1.2.3.4') returns true, and conversely allows 1.2.3.4 to match a whitelist entry of 1.2.3.40.
